articleTitle: Efficacy of distress tolerance techniques of dialectical behavior therapy in persons with obsessive-compulsive disorder: a study protocol for a randomized controlled trial.
takeaways: This is a study protocol describing a planned research trial, not completed research with findings, so no actionable takeaways about DTT effectiveness for OCD can be provided yet
The study will test distress tolerance techniques from dialectical behavior therapy as a potential alternative for OCD patients who struggle with standard exposure and response prevention therapy
Results are not available as this protocol was published in 2026 for a trial that has not been completed
Previous research shows DTT has helped with substance dependence, binge eating, and depression, but OCD applications remain unexplored
No quantitative outcomes, treatment durations, or efficacy measurements can be reported since this describes planned methodology rather than completed results
